description:
You are acting as a compliance-officer who has been handed an SQLite audit
    database and must quickly determine how many failed log-in attempts
    occurred during June 2024.  

    Preconditions already on the machine:
      • The database file is located at  
        /home/user/audit_data/compliance.db
      • Inside that database there is exactly one table named  
        system_access with this schema  
        ┌─────────┬───────────┬─────────────┬────────┐  
        │ id      │ username  │ access_time │ result │  
        └─────────┴───────────┴─────────────┴────────┘  
        access_time is stored as TEXT in the format  
        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS (24-hour clock).

    Required task:  
      1. Run ONE (1) terminal command that queries the database and counts
         every row whose  
           • result  = 'FAIL'   (exact, all caps) and  
           • access_time begins with the four characters “2024-06”.
      2. Redirect (or otherwise save) that single integer result into the file  
           /home/user/audit_logs/june_fail_count.log  
         The file must contain nothing except the integer and a single trailing
         newline character (Unix line-ending “\n”).  
         Example of correct file content if there were five failures:  
           5⏎   ←(newline)

    Output format that the automated checker will look for:
      • june_fail_count.log must exist.  
      • The file must contain exactly one line: an ASCII decimal integer
        followed by a single “\n”.  
      • For this dataset, the correct integer is 3.

    Notes & constraints:
      • Complete the task with a single sqlite3 invocation (no intermediate
        scripts).  
      • Do NOT modify the database contents.  
      • Create /home/user/audit_logs if it does not yet exist, using directory
        permissions that a normal user can write to (e.g. 755).  
      • You do not need root privileges.  
      • Apart from writing the required log file, no other persistent files
        should be created.
truth:
Database ground-truth (already present before the agent starts):

    File: /home/user/audit_data/compliance.db

    Table definition:
        CREATE TABLE system_access (
            id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            username    TEXT NOT NULL,
            access_time TEXT NOT NULL,   -- format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
            result      TEXT NOT NULL    -- either 'SUCCESS' or 'FAIL'
        );

    Pre-loaded rows (id, username, access_time, result):

        (1, 'alice',   '2024-05-20 09:10:00', 'SUCCESS')
        (2, 'bob',     '2024-06-02 14:55:00', 'FAIL')
        (3, 'charlie', '2024-06-10 07:32:12', 'FAIL')
        (4, 'dan',     '2024-06-15 15:45:30', 'FAIL')
        (5, 'erin',    '2024-05-18 22:11:09', 'FAIL')

    Verification expectations:

      • After successful completion, the file
        /home/user/audit_logs/june_fail_count.log must exist.

      • Its exact content must be:
            3\n     (ASCII character “3”, ASCII newline 0x0A)

      • No other differences (no extra spaces or lines).

    The automated test will open that file and compare it byte-for-byte
    against the expected string "3\n".
name: task_000000_ab7f4397
